zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan De Washbourne於大約1215年出生在英國時，她的父親Roger De Washbourne26歲，母親Joan De Wasseborne26歲。她於1245年之前與William de Odingsells在英國瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1268年之後在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Washbourne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Great and Little Washbourne (Gloucs) or Washbourne in Halwell (Devon). The variant Washburn could also be from Washburn (WR Yorks), but there is comparatively little evidence that this place yielded a surname.

variants.name: Washburn
meanings: English: habitational name from Great and Little Washbourne (Gloucestershire), or Washbourne in Halwell (Devon). The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wæsse (genitive wæssan) ‘wet place, swamp, marsh’ + burna ‘spring, stream’. The Devon placename derives from Old English wæsce ‘place for washing’ + burna.
History: John Washburn came to MA in 1626 and settled in Duxbury, MA, in 1632.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan De Washbourne was born about 1215, in England, her father, Roger De Washbourne, was 26 and her mother, Joan De Wasseborne, was 26. She married William de Odingsells before 1245, in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died after 1268, in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan De Washbourne est née environ 1215, à Angleterre, son père, Roger De Washbourne, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joan De Wasseborne, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé William de Odingsells avant 1245, à Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée après 1268, à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan De Washbourne um 1215 in England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger De Washbourne 26 und ihre Mutter Joan De Wasseborne 26. Sie hat vor 1245 in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England William de Odingsells geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb nach 1268 in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

Lei sposato William de Odingsells prima del 1245, nel Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì dopo il 1268, nel Warwick, Warwickshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan De Washbourne(이)가 대략 1215년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger De Washbourne(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan De Wasseborne(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1245년 이전 영국 워윅실르 맥스스토크에서 William de Odingsells(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1268년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan De Washbourne родилась около 1215 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Roger De Washbourne было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan De Wasseborne, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Odingsells перед 1245 г. в местности макссток, варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла после 1268 г. в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
